name: im-telling-the-truth-but-im-lying
description: Bassey Ikpi's 'I'm Telling the Truth, but I'm Lying: Essays' — a raw, poetic collection about growing up Nigerian-American, living with bipolar disorder, and learning to tell the truth even when it feels like lying.
useCase: Navigate mental health challenges, understand the Nigerian-American experience, find your voice through creative writing, and learn that healing happens one truth at a time.
knownRisks: Contains frank discussions of mental illness, hospitalization, and suicidal ideation
Memoir in essay form — not a clinical guide to bipolar disorder
Heavy emotional content — may be triggering for those in crisis
